# Install FreeLLMAPI for iii


[FreeLLMAPI](https://github.com/tashfeenahmed/freellmapi) is an open-source proxy that aggregates the
free tiers of 16+ LLM providers (Google, Groq, Cerebras, Mistral, OpenRouter, GitHub Models,
Cohere, Cloudflare, HuggingFace, NVIDIA, and more) behind a single **OpenAI-compatible** endpoint. It
tracks per-provider rate limits, fails over automatically, and stores keys encrypted (AES-256-GCM).

Because the endpoint implements the OpenAI API, any iii worker can use it as a drop-in LLM backend.
For example, an [agent worker](/tutorials/build-an-agent/overview) or a `content::classify` function
can call it without committing to a single paid provider.

<Info title="Separate projects">
  FreeLLMAPI is a standalone service, not part of the iii engine. You run it alongside iii (locally
  or on the same network) and call it over HTTP. Nothing in this guide modifies the engine.
</Info>

## 1. Install FreeLLMAPI

FreeLLMAPI ships as a Docker image. Pick one of the two paths below.

### Option A: Docker one-liner

```bash
curl -fsSL https://freellmapi.co/install.sh | bash
```

This creates `~/freellmapi`, generates an encryption key, pulls the image, and starts the container.
Re-running is safe; your `.env` and encryption key are preserved.

<Warning title="Review before piping to a shell">
  Inspect the script before running it if you have not used it before:
  `curl -fsSL https://freellmapi.co/install.sh | less`.
</Warning>

### Option B: Manual Docker Compose

**Prerequisites:** Docker, Docker Compose, and OpenSSL.

```bash
git clone https://github.com/tashfeenahmed/freellmapi.git
cd freellmapi

# Generate the encryption key used for at-rest credential storage
ENCRYPTION_KEY="$(openssl rand -hex 32)"
printf "ENCRYPTION_KEY=%s\nPORT=3001\n" "$ENCRYPTION_KEY" > .env

docker compose up -d
```

## 2. Add provider keys and mint a unified key

Open the dashboard at `http://localhost:3001`, add free-tier API
keys for the providers you want, and copy the **unified key** (it looks like
`freellmapi-...`). That single key is what your iii workers will use; FreeLLMAPI routes each request
to an available provider behind it.

## 3. Verify the endpoint

The gateway serves the OpenAI Chat Completions API at `http://localhost:3001/v1`:

```bash
curl http://localhost:3001/v1/chat/completions \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer freellmapi-your-unified-key" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"model":"auto","messages":[{"role":"user","content":"hi"}]}'
```

`"model": "auto"` lets FreeLLMAPI pick the best available model and fail over on rate limits. You can
also pin a specific provider/model string from the dashboard.

## 4. Call FreeLLMAPI from an iii worker

Treat the gateway as the worker's LLM backend. Install the OpenAI SDK alongside `iii-sdk`:

```bash
npm install iii-sdk openai
```

Register a function that proxies prompts through FreeLLMAPI. Read both the engine URL and the
gateway settings from the environment so nothing is hard-coded:

```typescript
import { registerWorker } from 'iii-sdk'
import OpenAI from 'openai'

const llm = new OpenAI({
  baseURL: process.env.FREELLMAPI_URL ?? 'http://localhost:3001/v1',
  apiKey: process.env.FREELLMAPI_KEY, // the freellmapi-... unified key
})

const worker = registerWorker(process.env.III_URL!, { workerName: 'llm' })

worker.registerFunction(
  'llm::complete',
  async (req, logger) => {
    const { prompt } = req.body as { prompt: string }
    logger.info('Calling FreeLLMAPI', { model: 'auto' })

    const completion = await llm.chat.completions.create({
      model: 'auto',
      messages: [{ role: 'user', content: prompt }],
    })

    return { status_code: 200, body: { text: completion.choices[0]?.message?.content ?? '' } }
  },
  { description: 'Generate a completion via the FreeLLMAPI gateway' },
)
```

Any other worker, or an agent, can now call `llm::complete` through the engine and reach every
provider FreeLLMAPI fronts, with rate-limit tracking and failover handled for you.

## Environment variables

FreeLLMAPI reads these (set them in its `.env`, not in iii):

| Variable | Purpose | Default |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `ENCRYPTION_KEY` | Required; encrypts stored provider keys | Generated on setup |
| `PORT` | Server / dashboard port | `3001` |
| `HOST_BIND` | Bind address; use `0.0.0.0` for LAN access | `127.0.0.1` |
| `FREELLMAPI_CONTEXT_HANDOFF` | Carry context across mid-conversation model switches | `off` |

On the iii side, the worker above expects `FREELLMAPI_URL` and `FREELLMAPI_KEY` (plus the usual
`III_URL`).

## Notes

- **Bind address.** FreeLLMAPI binds to `127.0.0.1` by default. If iii runs on another host or in a
  separate container network, set `HOST_BIND=0.0.0.0` and point `FREELLMAPI_URL` at the reachable
  address.
- **Free tiers have limits.** `model: "auto"` distributes requests across per-provider quotas, but
  sustained or latency-sensitive production traffic should still use a paid backend.
- **Keep the unified key secret.** Inject `FREELLMAPI_KEY` as an environment variable or secret;
  never commit it.
